channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: A 'Shark Tank' alum needed cash to pay tariffs. This shadowy lending world was ready
item.description: How about $350,000 within hours? The pitches flood small businesses: "No hidden fees, No BS." These financial lifelines are barely regulated and can t...

item.title: Four top U.S. speedskaters to watch at the Olympics
item.description: U.S. speed skaters set to compete in Milan are drawing comparisons to past greats like Eric Heiden, Bonnie Blair, and Apolo Ohno. Here are four to wat...

item.title: 5 glaring warning signs for Republicans in this year's midterm elections
item.description: Here's why Republicans are facing an uphill battle, particularly for retaining control of the House.

item.title: U.S. skater Connor McDermott-Mostowy joins record number of out LGBTQ Winter Olympians
item.description: When U.S. speedskater Connor McDermott-Mostowy makes his Winter Olympic debut in Milan, he'll join a record number of out LGBTQ athletes. But of the 4...

item.title: Need a new path in midlife? There's a school for that and a quiz to kickstart it
item.description: Schools across the country are offering courses and retreats for people 50+ who want to reinvent themselves and embrace lifelong learning and discover...

item.title: Hong Kong court jails media tycoon and British citizen Jimmy Lai for 20 years
item.description: Lai's supporters say this amounts to a death sentence, but authorities say it demonstrates the rule of law.

item.title: China sentences Hong Kong media mogul Jimmy Lai to 20 years in prison
item.description: Family of Jimmy Lai, a Hong Kong media tycoon and vocal critic of China's Communist rule, calls his prison sentence cruel, and the "end of justice."

item.title: Bad Bunny makes history as Trump criticises 'terrible' Super Bowl show
item.description: Puerto Rican artist Bad Bunny makes history with his entirely Spanish set on the sporting world's biggest stage.

item.title: Migrants languish in US detention centers facing dire conditions and prolonged waits
item.description: Prolonged detention has become more common in President Donald Trumpâs second term, at least partly because a new policy generally prohibits immigrati...

item.title: Eritrea accuses Ethiopia of fabricating report its troops had crossed the border
item.description: Ethiopia had alleged that Eritrean soldiers were on its territory and supporting rebels in the north.

item.title: "Black carbon" in Arctic an increasing concern amid other issues in region
item.description: "Black carbon," or soot, that spews from ships and makes the Artic Ocean ice melt even faster is an increasing concern in a region beset with many oth...

item.title: 'Super exciting!' - Seahawks fans celebrate Super Bowl win
item.description: The Seattle team produced a devastating defensive display to beat the New England Patriots.

item.title: Hospital flooded by Hurricane Helene to be rebuilt in another flood plain
item.description: Ballad Health, the nation's largest state-sanctioned hospital monopoly, plans to rebuild Unicoi County Hospital in Tennessee on land that two climate ...

item.title: 2/8: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Search for Nancy Guthrie reaches Day 8; Lindsey Vonn breaks leg in Olympic crash.

item.title: Trial against Meta in New Mexico focuses on dangers of child sexual exploitation on social media
Outrage as South Korean official suggests 'importing' foreign women to boost birth rate
item.description: Jindo county head Kim Hee-soo had suggested "importing young women from Vietnam or Sri Lanka" for young rural men.

item.title: Less than 14% of those ICE arrested had violent criminal records, data show
item.description: The official DHS statistics, which had not been previously reported, provide the most detailed look yet into who ICE has arrested during the Trump adm...

item.title: Investigation continues a week after Savannah Guthrie mother was reported missing
